title: Plus500 Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)

meta_description: Plus500 Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Plus500 (3.8★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Plus500 — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Plus500?

Plus500 is a regulated CFD broker that lets you trade forex, stocks, indices, commodities, cryptocurrencies, and ETFs through a proprietary web and mobile platform. Founded in 2008 and headquartered in Haifa, Israel, Plus500 is licensed by the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA), the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC), the Cyprus Securities and Exchange Commission (CySEC), and the Monetary Authority of Singapore (MAS)—giving it credibility with regulatory oversight from multiple top-tier authorities. With a 3.8/5 rating on TradingToolsHub, it's popular among beginner traders and mobile-first traders who want commission-free trading without the complexity of setting up third-party platforms. The broker charges no fees to open a demo or live account, making it accessible to traders at any experience level.

How to Create Your Plus500 Account

Getting started with Plus500 takes about 10–15 minutes from sign-up to first trade. Here's the process:

  • Visit the Plus500 website and click "Open Account" or download the mobile app from iOS or Android.
  • Enter your email and create a password. Use a strong password with mixed case, numbers, and symbols.
  • Verify your email address. Plus500 sends a confirmation link to your inbox. Click it to activate your account.
  • Complete the account registration form. You'll provide your full name, date of birth, phone number, residential address, and employment status.
  • Set up two-factor authentication (2FA). Use an authenticator app like Google Authenticator or Authy to secure your account.
  • Choose your base currency. Select USD, EUR, GBP, or another supported currency for your account.
  • Agree to terms and compliance documents. Read and accept Plus500's terms of service, privacy policy, and risk disclosures. Pay attention to the Conflicts of Interest statement—Plus500 is a market maker, so they profit when you lose.
  • Verify your identity (KYC). Upload a government-issued ID (passport, driver's license, or national ID) and a recent utility bill or bank statement showing your address. Most verifications complete within 24 hours.
  • Fund your account (optional for demo). If opening a live account, choose a funding method: credit/debit card, wire transfer, PayPal, or e-wallets. Deposits are instant for cards; wire transfers take 1–3 business days.

You'll get instant access to the demo account while verification is pending, so you can start practicing immediately.

Setting Up Plus500 for the First Time

Once you log in, customize your trading environment to match your workflow.

Dashboard Layout: The Plus500 platform uses a single-page design with your open positions on the left, available instruments in the center, and a mini chart on the right. Unlike multi-window platforms like MetaTrader, everything flows vertically—it's minimalist by design. Customize the sidebar by favoriting markets you trade frequently (e.g., EUR/USD, AAPL, BTC/USD).

Configure Alerts: Go to Settings → Alerts and set up price-based notifications. Click any instrument and set an alert for when it hits a specific price. Plus500 will notify you via the app, email, or SMS. This is essential if you trade in your spare time and can't watch charts all day.

Enable Guaranteed Stop-Loss: One of Plus500's standout features is guaranteed stop-loss orders, which protect you from slippage during overnight gaps or volatile news events. Go to Settings → Account → Risk Management and enable this. You'll pay a small premium (usually 0.5–2 pips) on orders that use guaranteed stops, but the protection is worth it for overnight positions.

Set Your Risk Per Trade: Before placing your first trade, decide your maximum loss tolerance per trade (e.g., 1–2% of account balance). Plus500's platform doesn't enforce this automatically, so you need discipline. Use their built-in stop-loss calculator to position-size correctly. Go to any instrument's trade ticket and scroll down to "Position Size Calculator."

Activate Performance Analytics: Go to Portfolio → Analytics to track your P&L, win rate, average win/loss, and Sharpe ratio. This auto-tracks all your trades and updates daily. Screenshot these stats monthly to identify weak setups and improve over time.

Link Your Broker (if applicable): If you own stocks elsewhere and want to hedge them with Plus500 CFDs, Plus500 doesn't have direct broker integration. However, you can manually track your external positions in a spreadsheet and mirror them with CFD positions on Plus500 for hedging.

Essential Features You Should Know

1. Paper Trading (Demo Account): Plus500 gives you an unlimited demo account with virtual $10,000. Use this to test your strategy, learn the platform, and build confidence without risking real money. Many traders keep the demo account running alongside their live account to test new ideas risk-free. Demo balances reset monthly if requested, so you can do fresh tests repeatedly.

2. Alerts: Set up price alerts, percentage-change alerts, and technical-level alerts across all markets. When EUR/USD hits 1.0950, you get a push notification, email, or SMS. This beats having to stare at charts and is perfect if you work a day job. Alerts are cumulative—you can set 50+ simultaneously without performance impact.

3. Mobile App: The Plus500 iOS and Android apps are consistently rated 4.5+ stars in app stores. They're not feature-parity with the web platform, but they include live charting, one-click trading, alerts, and portfolio tracking. Use the mobile app for monitoring positions while commuting; use the web platform for detailed analysis and setup.

4. Risk Management Tools: Beyond guaranteed stop-loss, Plus500 offers take-profit orders, trailing stops, and one-click closes. Trailing stops automatically lock in gains as a position moves in your favor—set a 50-pip trailing stop on a long EUR/USD position and it'll close if price reverses 50 pips from its peak. This is ideal for trending markets where you don't want to exit manually.

5. Performance Analytics: Every trade is logged with entry price, exit price, P&L, duration, and instrument. The analytics dashboard shows your monthly win rate, average winner size, largest loss, and Sharpe ratio. This data is gold for identifying which setups work and which don't. Export your trade history monthly and identify patterns.

6. News Feed: The platform includes an integrated financial news feed with real-time market updates, economic calendars, and earnings alerts. Before major events like Fed announcements, the news feed flags volatility expectations. Use this to avoid trading into earnings where volatility spikes unpredictably.

7. Education Resources: Plus500 offers webinars, video tutorials, and a beginner's guide to CFD trading. The webinar schedule is posted in the platform—most are 30–45 minutes and cover topics like risk management, technical analysis, and trading psychology. Free access for all account holders.

Plus500 Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

Plus500 has zero account opening fees and zero subscription fees across all account types. Here's what you're actually paying:

Demo Account: Completely free. $10,000 virtual balance. No fees. No time limit. Use it to practice indefinitely.

Live Account (Retail): Free to open and maintain. You pay via spreads (the bid-ask gap) on every trade. EUR/USD spreads are typically 1.0–1.5 pips; major indices like DAX have 2–3 pip spreads. You also pay a small premium (0.5–2 pips extra) if you use guaranteed stop-loss orders. An inactivity fee applies: if you don't trade for 3 consecutive months, Plus500 charges €10 per month until you resume activity. This is lower than competitors like IG and Saxo Bank.

Professional Account: Free to open if you qualify. You need €500K in assets under management or to work in the financial industry. As a pro, you get tighter spreads (0.5–1 pip on EUR/USD), lower or no inactivity fees, and access to higher leverage (up to 1:500 vs. 1:30 for retail). If you manage a fund or trade with substantial capital, professional status is worth applying for.

Which should you choose? Start with a demo account no matter your experience level. Once you've proven a profitable strategy over 50+ live trades, switch to a live account with micro-position sizes (e.g., 0.01 lots on forex). As your account grows to €500K+, upgrade to professional status to cut spreads in half.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Plus500

1. Use Guaranteed Stop-Loss on Overnight Positions: Overnight gaps can wipe out stops on uncontrolled-risk brokers. Plus500's guaranteed stop-loss costs 0.5–2 pips extra per trade but eliminates gap risk. On a 50-pip stop, paying 1 pip for protection is a 2% insurance cost—worth it for peace of mind.

2. Trade the Most Liquid Hours (3 AM–5 PM UTC): Plus500 spreads widen outside main market hours (Asian close, US pre-open). EUR/USD spreads might jump from 1.0 pip to 3–4 pips after 5 PM UTC when fewer traders are active. Plan major trades during the 8 AM–4 PM London time window when spreads are tightest.

3. Export Monthly Trade Stats and Identify Your A-Setup: The analytics dashboard shows your win rate by instrument. If you're 65% win rate on EUR/USD but only 40% on gold, stop trading gold and focus on EUR/USD. Most traders fail by diversifying too much. Find your one edge and repeat it.

4. Set Position Limits in Your Strategy Before Opening Live: Plus500 doesn't enforce maximum position size rules. You could accidentally place a 10-lot trade and blow your account in minutes. Before going live, write down your position size rules on paper: "Max 5 lots per trade, max 10 lots open at once, max 5% of account risk per trade." Tape it to your monitor.

5. Use Trailing Stops on Trending Markets: On a strong downtrend, set a trailing stop 100 pips above your entry. As price drops, the stop trails down automatically, locking in profit on every new low. This removes emotion from exiting winners early and lets winners run.

6. Test New Ideas on Demo for 50+ Trades Before Going Live: Your demo account is your laboratory. Spend 3–4 weeks testing any new strategy on demo with real price action. Once you hit 50+ trades with a positive expectancy (win rate × avg winner > loss rate × avg loser), then risk real money.

7. Monitor Weekly Inactivity: Set a calendar reminder every 2 months to place at least one trade (even 0.01 lot micro trades count). Missing 3 months triggers the €10/month inactivity fee. One tiny trade every 8 weeks costs almost nothing and keeps you exempt.

Common Plus500 Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue: Your trade closed automatically at 2 AM, but you didn't set a stop. Plus500 liquidates positions to protect itself from negative balance risk. If your account equity dips near zero, the system force-closes positions to prevent you owing the broker money. This typically happens to over-leveraged retail traders on overnight gaps. Solution: Keep at least 50% of your balance as margin buffer. Don't use the full 1:30 leverage offered to retail.

Issue: Spreads on your instrument just widened from 2 pips to 10+ pips. You're probably trading outside main market hours or a low-liquidity instrument. EUR/USD spreads stay tight 24/5, but minor pairs and commodities gap during Asian hours. Solution: Check the current bid-ask spread before placing limit orders. If it's unusually wide, cancel the order and wait for London open.

Issue: Your email alerts aren't arriving. Check three things: (1) Verify alerts are enabled in Settings → Alerts; (2) Check your spam folder—alerts sometimes get filtered; (3) Ensure push notifications are allowed on your device. Solution: Test with a price alert 5 pips away from current price to confirm the system works, then adjust your primary alerts.

Issue: You forgot your password and can't log in. Click "Forgot Password" on the login screen. Plus500 sends a reset link to your registered email within 2 minutes. If the email doesn't arrive, check spam. If you still can't access your account, contact Plus500 support (live chat available 24/5 on the website).

Is Plus500 Worth It? Our Verdict

Plus500 is best for beginner and intermediate traders who want commission-free CFD trading on mobile with zero account fees and a simple, distraction-free platform. The FCA/ASIC/CySEC regulation provides real legal protection, guaranteed stop-loss orders are a rare competitive advantage, and the mobile app quality is genuinely excellent. However, it's not ideal if you need MetaTrader 4/5 for automated strategies, custom indicators, or complex charting—Plus500's proprietary platform has limited technical analysis tools. Compare with Interactive Brokers if you want MetaTrader access and algorithmic trading, or Saxo Bank if you need advanced charting tools. For straightforward CFD trading with a 3.8/5 rating and zero fees, Plus500 delivers solid value, especially for traders who use mobile apps more than desktop. The 3-month inactivity fee is negligible if you're an active trader, and the €10/month penalty for dormant accounts is lower than industry averages. Open a demo account today, test your strategy on virtual money for 50+ trades, and if your win rate stays positive, fund a small live account. Start micro and scale up.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
